rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER JEW VS. KENNETH JEW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Of Special Mas Thomas Tsang For Order Authorizing Payment Of Fees And Reimbursement Of Expenses, Authorizing, Disbursement Of Funds, And Discharging Special Mater
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 20, 2010, line 1. OTHER THOMAS TSANG'S Motion For an Order Authorizing Payment Of Fees And Reimbursement Of Expenses, Authorizing, Disbursement Of Funds, And Discharging Special Master. GRANTED IN PART. The court approves fees and reinbursements in the amount of $95,714.98. Court Denies the request for attorney fees. Special Master/Receiver did not obtain approval of the court. CRC 3.1180. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RITA O'FLYNN VS. BEYOND CHRON (A CA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 20, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANTS RANDY SHAW AND BEYONDCHRON'S Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs. MOTION GRANTED IN PART. REASONABLE AND NECESSARY ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $21,326.10. THE COURT DISALLOWS THE FEES ASSOCIATED WITH THE LEGAL INTERN. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT AT THE TIME THE INTERN'S HOURS WERE PERFORMED, THE INTERN WAS BILLED AT ANY RATE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RONNEY BECKER VS. ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 20, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANT PHILIP BIRKMYER'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RONNEY BECKER VS. ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 20, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANT PHILIP BIRKMYER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 3rd. and 5th. causes of action wich plaintiff agrees are not directed at Birkmyer. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 4th. cause of action for plaintiff to plead whether contract was oral, written or implied by conduct; Sustained with leave to amend as to 9th. cause of action for plaintiff to allege that plaintiff has paid more than his fair share, or that he has in fact paid anything on the debts. Overruled as to remainder of complaint. =(302/CWW)
